```{r}
                   mention_country_confidence <  0.70], 100, "conf_medium"),
  
  # High tier (syntax / org-embedded)
  take(person_rows[mention_country_confidence >= 0.70 & 
                   mention_country_confidence <  0.85], 150, "conf_high"),
  
  # Very high tier (org exact match)
  take(person_rows[mention_country_confidence >= 0.85], 100, "conf_very_high"),
  
  # NA rows for recall estimation
  take(na_rows, 250, "na_recall")
)

# Combine
validation_sample_strat <- rbindlist(strata)[, .(
  orig_row_id,
  text_en,
  person,
  mention_country,
  country_final,
  mention_country_confidence,
  mention_country_sources,   # lowercase c — check your column name
  stratum                   # keep this for per-stratum accuracy reporting
)]

# Verify stratum sizes (some may be smaller if data is limited)
validation_sample_strat[, .N, by = stratum]


write.csv(
  validation_sample_strat,
  "validation_sample_strat.csv",
  row.names = FALSE,
  fileEncoding = "UTF-8"
)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)


stages <- list(
  "Raw pipeline\n(pre-filter)" = data.frame(
    True = c("Non-expert", "Non-expert", "Expert", "Expert"),
    Predicted = c("Extracted", "Missed", "Extracted", "Missed"),
    n = c(218, 228, 532, 22),
    label = c("FP\n218", "TN\n~228", "TP\n532", "FN\n~22"),
    correct = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE)
  ),
  "After manual\nfiltering" = data.frame(
    True = c("Non-expert", "Non-expert", "Expert", "Expert"),
    Predicted = c("Extracted", "Missed", "Extracted", "Missed"),
    n = c(83, 365, 530, 22),
    label = c("FP\n~83", "TN\n~365", "TP\n530", "FN\n~22"),
    correct = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE)
  )
)

precision_vals <- c(
  "Raw pipeline\n(pre-filter)" = "Precision = 71%  |  Recall ≈ 91%  |  F1 ≈ 81%",
  "After manual\nfiltering"   = "Precision = 87%  |  Recall ≈ 91%"
)

plot_data <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(stages), function(s) {
    stages[[s]] %>% mutate(stage = s)
  })
) %>%
  mutate(
    stage = factor(stage, levels = names(stages)),
    True = factor(True, levels = c("Non-expert", "Expert")),
    Predicted = factor(Predicted, levels = c("Extracted", "Missed")),
    prec_label = precision_vals[as.character(stage)]
  )

label_data <- plot_data %>%
  distinct(stage, prec_label) %>%
  mutate(
    True = factor("Expert", levels = c("Non-expert", "Expert")),
    Predicted = factor("Extracted", levels = c("Extracted", "Missed"))
  )

fig1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Predicted, y = True, fill = correct)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    size = 4.2,
    fontface = "bold",
    colour = "white",
    lineheight = 1.3
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_data,
    aes(label = prec_label),
    x = 1.5,
    y = 0.35,
    size = 3.0,
    colour = "grey30",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~stage, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "grey30", "FALSE" = "grey70"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    x = "Pipeline output",
    y = "True classification",
    title = "Expert extraction performance"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey30"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)
    ),
    panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines")
  )

fig1
ggsave("fig_cm_precision.pdf", fig1, width = 8, height = 4.5, device = cairo_pdf)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

fd_data <- data.frame(
  True = factor(
    c("Domestic\n(Russian)", "Domestic\n(Russian)",
      "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)", "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)"),
    levels = c("Domestic\n(Russian)", "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)")
  ),
  Predicted = factor(
    c("Domestic\n(Russian)", "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)",
      "Domestic\n(Russian)", "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)"),
    levels = c("Domestic\n(Russian)", "Foreign\n(Non-Russian)")
  ),
  n = c(210, 111, 25, 186),
  correct = c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE),
  label = c(
    "TN\n210\n(65% recall)",
    "FN\n111\nTopic contamination:\nRussian experts in\nforeign-topic articles",
    "FP\n25",
    "TP\n186\n(88% recall)"
  )
)

# Summary metrics for annotation
metrics <- data.frame(
  x = 1.5,
  y = c(0.35, 0.20, 0.05),
  label = c(
    "Overall accuracy: 74%",
    "Foreign precision: 63%",
    "n = 532 valid experts"
  )
)

fig2 <- ggplot(fd_data, aes(x = Predicted, y = True, fill = correct)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = label),
    size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", colour = "white", lineheight = 1.2
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = metrics$x, y = metrics$y,
    label = metrics$label,
    size = 3.2, colour = "grey30"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#2166ac", "FALSE" = "#d73027"),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    x = "Pipeline classification",
    y = "True classification",
    title = "Foreign/domestic classification accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12, hjust = 0.5)
  )

fig2
ggsave("fig_cm_fd.pdf", fig2, width = 7, height = 5.5, device = cairo_pdf)
```

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

cal_data <- data.frame(
  tier = c(
    "Corpus-only\n(Russia, conf=0)",
    "High\n(conf ≥ 0.85)",
    "Medium\n(conf 0.70–0.84)",
    "Corpus-only\n(Ukraine, conf=0)",
    "Low\n(conf 0.55–0.69)",
    "Corpus-only\n(Other foreign, conf=0)"
  ),
  accuracy = c(0.95, 0.81, 0.69, 0.63, 0.69, 0.54),
  n = c(150, 100, 150, 150, 100, 100),
  tier_type = c(
    "Corpus-only", "Explicit signal", "Explicit signal",
    "Corpus-only", "Explicit signal", "Corpus-only"
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    se = sqrt(accuracy * (1 - accuracy) / n),
    ci_lo = accuracy - 1.96 * se,
    ci_hi = accuracy + 1.96 * se,
    tier = factor(tier, levels = tier[order(accuracy)])
  )

fig3 <- ggplot(cal_data, aes(x = accuracy, y = tier, colour = tier_type)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.74, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.81, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "#2166ac", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi),
    height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.745, y = 0.5,
    label = "Overall\n74%",
    hjust = 0, size = 3, colour = "grey40"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text", x = 0.815, y = 0.5,
    label = "Robustness\nthreshold\n81%",
    hjust = 0, size = 3, colour = "#2166ac"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 0.96,
    y = "Corpus-only\n(Russia, conf=0)",
    label = "Corpus accumulation\nmore reliable than\nsingle-mention extraction",
    hjust = 1, size = 2.8, colour = "grey30", fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c("Corpus-only" = "#d73027", "Explicit signal" = "#2166ac"),
    name = "Signal type"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0.45, 1.0),
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    breaks = seq(0.5, 1.0, 0.1)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "FD classification accuracy",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Classification accuracy by confidence tier",
    subtitle = "Error bars show 95% binomial confidence intervals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40")
  )

fig3

ggsave("fig_calibration.pdf", fig3, width = 8, height = 5.5, device = cairo_pdf)

```

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

# Confusion matrix values
cm_data <- tibble(
  Predicted = factor(c("Expert", "Expert", "No expert", "No expert"),
                     levels = c("Expert", "No expert")),
  Actual    = factor(c("Expert", "No expert", "Expert", "No expert"),
                     levels = c("Expert", "No expert")),
  n         = c(695, 55, 16, 234),
  label     = c("TP = 695", "FP = 55", "FN = 16", "TN = 234")
)

# Precision, recall, F1 for subtitle
precision <- 695 / (695 + 55)
recall    <- 695 / (695 + 16)
f1        <- 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall)

subtitle_text <- sprintf(
  "Precision = %.1f%%   Recall = %.1f%%   F1 = %.3f",
  precision * 100, recall * 100, f1
)

ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = Predicted, y = Actual, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "grey30",
                      name = "Count") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "Expert extraction: confusion matrix",
    subtitle = subtitle_text,
    x        = "Predicted",
    y        = "Actual"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40"),
    axis.text     = element_text(size = 12),
     legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid    = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("plot1_person_confusion_matrix.png", width = 6, height = 4.5)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

plot_data <- tribble(
  ~stratum,             ~tier_label,               ~person_acc, ~country_acc, ~fd_acc, ~n_confirmed,
  "conf0_Russia",       "conf = 0\n(Russia)",       94.0,        50.0,         50.0,    6,
  "conf0_Ukraine",      "conf = 0\n(Ukraine)",      94.0,         3.6,          4.5,    56,
  "conf0_other",        "conf = 0\n(other)",        87.0,         0.0,          0.0,    19,
  "conf_medium",        "conf 0.55-0.70\n(medium)", 92.0,        88.0,         90.0,    92,
  "conf_high",          "conf 0.70-0.85\n(high)",   93.3,        86.4,         89.6,   140,
  "conf_very_high",     "conf >= 0.85\n(very high)",94.0,        86.2,         90.0,    94,
)

plot_long <- plot_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(person_acc, country_acc, fd_acc),
               names_to  = "metric",
               values_to = "accuracy") %>%
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
                    "person_acc"  = "Person extraction",
                    "country_acc" = "Country attribution",
                    "fd_acc"      = "Foreign / domestic"),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Person extraction",
                                       "Country attribution",
                                       "Foreign / domestic")),
    low_n      = n_confirmed < 30,
    tier_label = factor(tier_label, levels = plot_data$tier_label)
  )

solid_data  <- plot_long %>% filter(!low_n)
dotted_data <- plot_long %>% filter(low_n)

ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = tier_label, y = accuracy,
                      colour = metric, group = metric)) +
  geom_line(data = solid_data,  linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "solid") +
  geom_line(data = dotted_data, linewidth = 0.8, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = low_n), size = 3.5, fill = "white", stroke = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 85, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 5.8, y = 87,
           label = "85%", size = 3.2, colour = "grey50") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = 16, `TRUE` = 21),
                     guide = "none") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Person extraction"  = "#1a5276",
                                 "Country attribution" = "#c0392b",
                                 "Foreign / domestic"  = "#1e8449"),
                      name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  labs(
    title    = "Validation accuracy by confidence tier",
    subtitle = "Open points indicate fewer than 30 confirmed cases (high uncertainty)",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Accuracy (confirmed cases only)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 9),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 9)
  )

ggsave("plot2_accuracy_by_confidence.pdf", width = 8, height = 5)
```

```{r}

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

# ── shared data ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

all_data <- tribble(
  ~tier_label,                ~low_n, ~metric,              ~accuracy,
  "conf = 0\n(Russia)",       TRUE,   "Person extraction",   94.0,
  "conf = 0\n(Russia)",       TRUE,   "Country attribution", 50.0,
  "conf = 0\n(Russia)",       TRUE,   "Foreign / domestic",  50.0,
  "conf = 0\n(Ukraine)",      FALSE,  "Person extraction",   94.0,
  "conf = 0\n(Ukraine)",      FALSE,  "Country attribution",  3.6,
  "conf = 0\n(Ukraine)",      FALSE,  "Foreign / domestic",   4.5,
  "conf = 0\n(other)",        FALSE,  "Person extraction",   87.0,
  "conf = 0\n(other)",        FALSE,  "Country attribution",  0.0,
  "conf = 0\n(other)",        FALSE,  "Foreign / domestic",   0.0,
  "conf 0.55-0.70\n(medium)", FALSE,  "Person extraction",   92.0,
  "conf 0.55-0.70\n(medium)", FALSE,  "Country attribution", 88.0,
  "conf 0.55-0.70\n(medium)", FALSE,  "Foreign / domestic",  90.0,
  "conf 0.70-0.85\n(high)",   FALSE,  "Person extraction",   93.3,
  "conf 0.70-0.85\n(high)",   FALSE,  "Country attribution", 86.4,
  "conf 0.70-0.85\n(high)",   FALSE,  "Foreign / domestic",  89.6,
  "conf >= 0.85\n(very high)",FALSE,  "Person extraction",   94.0,
  "conf >= 0.85\n(very high)",FALSE,  "Country attribution", 86.2,
  "conf >= 0.85\n(very high)",FALSE,  "Foreign / domestic",  90.0,
) %>%
  mutate(
    metric     = factor(metric, levels = c("Person extraction",
                                           "Country attribution",
                                           "Foreign / domestic")),
    tier_label = factor(tier_label, levels = unique(tier_label))
  )

high_data <- all_data %>%
  filter(!str_detect(tier_label, "conf = 0")) %>%
  mutate(tier_label = factor(tier_label,
                             levels = c("conf 0.55-0.70\n(medium)",
                                        "conf 0.70-0.85\n(high)",
                                        "conf >= 0.85\n(very high)")))

colours <- c("Person extraction"  = "#1a5276",
             "Country attribution"= "#c0392b",
             "Foreign / domestic" = "#1e8449")

# ── Option B: dot plot, all tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────

plotB <- ggplot(all_data,
                aes(x = accuracy, y = tier_label, colour = metric, shape = low_n)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 85, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 86, y = 0.55, label = "85%",
           size = 3, colour = "grey50", hjust = 0) +
  geom_point(size = 4, stroke = 1.1,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(`TRUE` = 1, `FALSE` = 16),
                     labels = c(`TRUE`  = "Fewer than 30 confirmed cases",
                                `FALSE` = "30+ confirmed cases"),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = colours, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  labs(
    title    = "Validation accuracy by confidence tier",
    subtitle = "Hollow points indicate fewer than 30 confirmed cases",
    x        = "Accuracy (confirmed cases only)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 9),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.box       = "vertical",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

plotB

ggsave("plotB_dot.pdf", plotB, width = 7, height = 5.5)


# ── Option C: dot plot, high-confidence tiers only ────────────────────────────

plotC <- ggplot(high_data,
                aes(x = accuracy, y = tier_label, colour = metric)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 85, linetype = "dashed",
             colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = 86, y = 0.55, label = "85%",
           size = 3, colour = "grey50", hjust = 0) +
  geom_point(size = 4.5,
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.4)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(accuracy, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.4),
            hjust = -0.35, size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = colours, name = NULL) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(75, 100),
                     breaks = seq(75, 100, 5),
                     labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +
  labs(
    title    = "Validation accuracy at conf \u2265 0.55",
    subtitle = "Confirmed cases only. Low-confidence (conf = 0) strata excluded.",
    x        = "Accuracy",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 9),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

plotC
ggsave("plotC_dot_highconf.pdf", plotC, width = 6.5, height = 4)
```

```{r}
final_data_verify %>%
  mutate(
    # How was country_final determined?
    final_tier = case_when(
      is.na(person)                                      ~ "No extraction",
      country_final == "Unknown" | country_final == "" | 
        is.na(country_final)                             ~ "Unknown after overwrite",
      # Was it overwritten? i.e. mention_country_confidence == 0 
      # but country_final is not Unknown
      mention_country_confidence == 0 & 
        !is.na(country_final) & 
        country_final != "Unknown"                       ~ "conf = 0 (rescued by majority vote)",
      mention_country_confidence >= 0.55 & 
        mention_country_confidence < 0.70                ~ "conf 0.55-0.70",
      mention_country_confidence >= 0.70 & 
        mention_country_confidence < 0.85                ~ "conf 0.70-0.85",
      mention_country_confidence >= 0.85                 ~ "conf >= 0.85",
      TRUE                                               ~ "other"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(final_tier) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print()

final_data_verify %>%
  filter(mention_country_confidence == 0 & 
         !is.na(country_final) & 
         country_final != "Unknown") %>%
  count(country_final) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  print(n = 20)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)

# Confusion matrix values
# Restricted to conf_medium, conf_high, conf_very_high confirmed rows
# (conf0 strata excluded as they are not in the final analysis dataset)
cm_data <- tibble(
  Predicted = factor(c("Foreign", "Foreign", "Domestic", "Domestic"),
                     levels = c("Foreign", "Domestic")),
  Actual    = factor(c("Foreign", "Domestic", "Foreign", "Domestic"),
                     levels = c("Foreign", "Domestic")),
  n         = c(129, 25, 7, 154),
  label     = c("TP = 129", "FP = 25", "FN = 7", "TN = 154")
)

precision <- 129 / (129 + 25)
recall    <- 129 / (129 + 7)
f1        <- 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall)
accuracy  <- (129 + 154) / (129 + 25 + 7 + 154)

subtitle_text <- sprintf(
  "Precision = %.1f%%   Recall = %.1f%%   Accuracy = %.1f%%   F1 = %.3f",
  precision * 100, recall * 100, accuracy * 100, f1
)

ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = Predicted, y = Actual, fill = n)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "grey30") +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "Foreign/domestic classification: confusion matrix",
    subtitle = subtitle_text,
    x        = "Predicted",
    y        = "Actual"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, colour = "grey40", size = 9),
    plot.caption  = element_text(colour = "grey50", size = 8, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text     = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid    = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("plot3_fd_confusion_matrix.png", width = 6.5, height = 5)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)

# ── Top 10 foreign experts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
top_foreign <- expert_topic_sentiment_master %>%
  filter(is_foreign_final == TRUE) %>%
  count(person, country_final, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  mutate(person = fct_reorder(person, n))

# ── Top 10 domestic experts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
top_domestic <- expert_topic_sentiment_master %>%
  filter(is_foreign_final == FALSE) %>%
  count(person, country_final, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  mutate(person = fct_reorder(person, n))

# ── Plot foreign ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_foreign <- ggplot(top_foreign,
                    aes(x = n, y = person, fill = country_final)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(n)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
                     labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "Country") +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 10 most cited foreign experts",
    subtitle = "By total number of mentions in the analysis dataset",
    x        = "Number of mentions",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9, hjust = 0),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

p_foreign
ggsave("plot_top10_foreign.pdf", p_foreign, width = 7, height = 5)

# ── Plot domestic ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_domestic <- ggplot(top_domestic,
                     aes(x = n, y = person)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, fill = "#2c7bb6") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(n)),
            hjust = -0.15, size = 3.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
                     labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 10 most cited domestic experts",
    subtitle = "By total number of mentions in the analysis dataset",
    x        = "Number of mentions",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(colour = "grey40", size = 9, hjust = 0),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank()
  )

p_domestic
ggsave("plot_top10_domestic.pdf", p_domestic, width = 7, height = 5)
```

```{r}


```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Define geopolitical topics
geo_topics <- c(
  "Ukraine war",
  "International geopolitics",
  "Military technology",
  "Energy and sanctions"
)

# Prepare data
topic_counts <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "Other / no clear topic") %>%
  group_by(dominant_topic_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    foreign_expert_count = sum(foreign_expert, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_group = if_else(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% geo_topics,
      "Geopolitical",
      "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(foreign_expert_count)) %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = factor(
      dominant_topic_clean,
      levels = rev(dominant_topic_clean)
    )
  )

# Plot
ggplot(topic_counts, aes(x = foreign_expert_count, y = dominant_topic_clean)) +
  
  geom_col(aes(fill = topic_group), width = 0.7) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Geopolitical" = "grey20",
      "Other" = "grey70"
    ),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  
  labs(
    x = "Number of articles with foreign expert",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Raw count of foreign expert use across topics"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```

## Manual validation subtest

```{r}

set.seed(3.14)  # optional, for reproducibility
sample_50 <- validation_results[sample(nrow(validation_results), 50), ]


write.csv(sample_50, "sample50.csv")
```

### Expert frequency robustness: h1

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Data preparation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "Other / no clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = relevel(
      factor(dominant_topic_clean),
      ref = "Courts and crime"
    ),
    post_2014 = as.integer(year >= 2014),
    post_2022 = as.integer(year >= 2022)
  )

h1_data_restricted <- h1_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85)

# ── Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_m1 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

h1_m2 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

h1_m4 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
             data   = h1_data_restricted,
             family = binomial)

h1_m5 <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + post_2014 + post_2022,
             data   = h1_data,
             family = binomial)

# ── Coefficient map in block order ─────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map <- c(
  # Geopolitical block
  "dominant_topic_cleanInternational geopolitics" = "International geopolitics",
  "dominant_topic_cleanUkraine war"               = "Ukraine war",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMilitary technology"       = "Military technology",
  "dominant_topic_cleanEnergy and sanctions"      = "Energy and sanctions",
  # Domestic block
  "dominant_topic_cleanMacroeconomy"              = "Macroeconomy",
  "dominant_topic_cleanOpinion and commentary"    = "Opinion and commentary",
  "dominant_topic_cleanInformation services"      = "Information services",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMedia and society"         = "Media and society",
  "dominant_topic_cleanKremlin governance"        = "Kremlin governance",
  "dominant_topic_cleanHousing market"            = "Housing market",
  "dominant_topic_cleanDevelopment and education" = "Development and education",
  "dominant_topic_cleanCOVID-19 and health"       = "COVID-19 and health",
  "dominant_topic_cleanGeneral news"              = "General news",
  "dominant_topic_cleanRegional and local issues" = "Regional and local issues",
  # Controls
  "year"      = "Year (linear)",
  "post_2014" = "Post-2014",
  "post_2022" = "Post-2022"
)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "No controls"              = h1_m1,
    "+ Year (linear)"          = h1_m2,
    "Restricted (conf. 0.85+)" = h1_m4,
    "+ Era indicators"         = h1_m5
  ),
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = coef_map,
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "H1: Foreign expert presence across topics",
  notes      = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic, domestic expert.",
    "Restricted column uses observations where expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85.",
    "Era indicators: Post-2014 and Post-2022 are binary variables marking",
    "the editorial transformation and the full-scale invasion respectively.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Geopolitical topics",
    start_row  = 1,
    end_row    = 4,
    bold       = TRUE,
    background = "#e8f0f7"
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Domestic / other topics",
    start_row  = 5,
    end_row    = 14,
    bold       = TRUE,
    background = "#f7f0e8"
  ) %>%
  pack_rows(
    "Controls",
    start_row  = 15,
    end_row    = 17,
    bold       = FALSE,
    background = "#f5f5f5"
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "           = 1,
      "Main models" = 2,
      "Robustness"  = 2)
  ) %>%
  column_spec(4, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(5, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)


library(broom)

# Extract tidy results from all three models
m1_tidy <- tidy(h1_m1) %>% mutate(model = "No controls")
m2_tidy <- tidy(h1_m2) %>% mutate(model = "+ Year")
m4_tidy <- tidy(h1_m4) %>% mutate(model = "Robustness")

all_results <- bind_rows(m1_tidy, m2_tidy, m4_tidy) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, p.value)

print(all_results, n = 100)

summary(h1_m2)
summary(h1_m4)

# ── Mention frequency robustness ───────────────────────────────────────────

# Count unique articles per expert (not raw mentions)
expert_article_counts <- master_dataset %>%
  distinct(expert_id, row_id) %>%
  count(expert_id, name = "n_articles")

# Join back to h1_data
h1_data <- h1_data %>%
  left_join(expert_article_counts, by = "expert_id") %>%
  mutate(
    inv_freq_weight = 1 / n_articles,
    freq_bin = case_when(
      n_articles == 1        ~ "Single mention",
      n_articles <= 5        ~ "2-5 articles",
      n_articles <= 20       ~ "6-20 articles",
      TRUE                   ~ "20+ articles"
    )
  )

# Model 1: Restricted to experts appearing in 5 or fewer articles
h1_data_rare <- h1_data %>% filter(n_articles <= 5)

h1_m_rare <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                 data   = h1_data_rare,
                 family = binomial)

# Model 2: Inverse frequency weighted
h1_m_weighted <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                     data    = h1_data,
                     family  = binomial,
                     weights = inv_freq_weight)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main"                     = h1_m2,
    "Rare experts (≤5 articles)" = h1_m_rare,
    "Inverse frequency weighted" = h1_m_weighted
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Mention frequency",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "Mention frequency counted as distinct articles per expert_id.",
    "Rare experts column restricts to experts appearing in 5 or fewer articles.",
    "Weighted column uses inverse article frequency as observation weight.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",    1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",              15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5")

```

### Expert Frequency robustness: H2

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Data preparation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h2_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "Other / no clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_block = factor(
      if_else(topic_group == "geopolitics", "Geopolitical", "Domestic / other"),
      levels = c("Domestic / other", "Geopolitical")
    )
  )

h2_data_restricted <- h2_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85)

# ── Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Main models
h2_aw  <- glm(anti_west            ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)
h2_au  <- glm(anti_ukraine         ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)
h2_puk <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)
h2_ar  <- glm(anti_russia          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)
h2_pw  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)
h2_pr  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
              data = h2_data, family = binomial)

# Robustness models
h2_aw_r  <- glm(anti_west            ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)
h2_au_r  <- glm(anti_ukraine         ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)
h2_puk_r <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)
h2_ar_r  <- glm(anti_russia          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)
h2_pw_r  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)
h2_pr_r  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                data = h2_data_restricted, family = binomial)

# ── Raw outcome counts for footer ─────────────────────────────────────────

outcome_counts <- h2_data %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(anti_west,            na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(anti_ukraine,         na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(anti_russia,          na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,    na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,  na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (full sample): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    outcome_counts$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", outcome_counts$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  outcome_counts$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  outcome_counts$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     outcome_counts$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   outcome_counts$pr,  "."
)

# ── Coefficient map ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map <- c(
  "foreign_expert"          = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "year"                    = "Year"
)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_r,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_r,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_r,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_r,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_r,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_r
  ),
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = coef_map,
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "H2: Foreign expert evaluative profile across sentiment targets",
  notes      = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Robustness columns restricted to expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85.",
    counts_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                          = 1,
      "Main models"                = 6,
      "Robustness (conf. 0.85+)"   = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

library(broom)

models_main <- list(
  anti_west    = h2_aw,
  anti_ukraine = h2_au,
  pro_ukraine  = h2_puk,
  anti_russia  = h2_ar,
  pro_west     = h2_pw,
  pro_russia   = h2_pr
)

models_rob <- list(
  anti_west_r    = h2_aw_r,
  anti_ukraine_r = h2_au_r,
  pro_ukraine_r  = h2_puk_r,
  anti_russia_r  = h2_ar_r,
  pro_west_r     = h2_pw_r,
  pro_russia_r   = h2_pr_r
)

all_h2 <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(models_main), function(n)
    tidy(models_main[[n]]) %>% mutate(model = n)),
  lapply(names(models_rob), function(n)
    tidy(models_rob[[n]]) %>% mutate(model = n))
) %>%
  filter(term %in% c("foreign_expert",
                     "topic_blockGeopolitical",
                     "year")) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, p.value)

print(all_h2, n = 100)

print(outcome_counts)

# ── Mention frequency robustness for H2 ───────────────────────────────────

# Reuse expert_article_counts from H1 section
# (if running independently, recompute here)
expert_article_counts <- master_dataset %>%
  distinct(expert_id, row_id) %>%
  count(expert_id, name = "n_articles")

h2_data <- h2_data %>%
  left_join(expert_article_counts, by = "expert_id") %>%
  mutate(inv_freq_weight = 1 / n_articles)

# Rare experts dataset
h2_data_rare <- h2_data %>% filter(n_articles <= 5)

# ── Rare experts models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h2_aw_rare  <- glm(anti_west            ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)
h2_au_rare  <- glm(anti_ukraine         ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)
h2_puk_rare <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)
h2_ar_rare  <- glm(anti_russia          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)
h2_pw_rare  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)
h2_pr_rare  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                   data = h2_data_rare, family = binomial)

# ── Inverse frequency weighted models ─────────────────────────────────────

h2_aw_wt  <- glm(anti_west            ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)
h2_au_wt  <- glm(anti_ukraine         ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)
h2_puk_wt <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)
h2_ar_wt  <- glm(anti_russia          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)
h2_pw_wt  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)
h2_pr_wt  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data, family = binomial, weights = inv_freq_weight)

# ── Outcome counts for rare sample ────────────────────────────────────────

counts_rare <- h2_data_rare %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(anti_west,            na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(anti_ukraine,         na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(anti_russia,          na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,    na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,  na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_rare_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (rare experts sample, n_articles <= 5): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    counts_rare$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", counts_rare$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  counts_rare$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  counts_rare$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     counts_rare$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   counts_rare$pr,  "."
)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_rare,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_rare,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_rare,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_rare,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_rare,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_rare,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_wt,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_wt,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_wt,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_wt,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_wt,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_wt
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Mention frequency",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Rare experts column restricts to experts appearing in 5 or fewer articles.",
    "Weighted column uses inverse article frequency as observation weight.",
    counts_note,
    counts_rare_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 10
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                            = 1,
      "Main models"                  = 6,
      "Rare experts (≤5 articles)"   = 6,
      "Inverse frequency weighted"   = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13,  background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(14:19, background = "#f0f0f0", italic = TRUE)
```

### Expert Frequency robustness: H3

```{r}

library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)
# ── Data preparation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
h3_data <- master_dataset %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "Other / no clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    topic_block = factor(
      if_else(topic_group == "geopolitics", "Geopolitical", "Domestic / other"),
      levels = c("Domestic / other", "Geopolitical")
    )
  )
h3_data_restricted <- h3_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85)
# ── Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
h3_m1 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block,
             data   = h3_data,
             family = binomial)
h3_m2 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
             data   = h3_data,
             family = binomial)
h3_m3 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
             data   = h3_data,
             family = binomial)
h3_m4 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
             data   = h3_data_restricted,
             family = binomial)
# ── Coefficient map ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
coef_map <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                        = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical"               = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Foreign expert x Geopolitical",
  "year"                                  = "Year"
)
# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
modelsummary(
  list(
    "No controls"          = h3_m1,
    "+ Year"               = h3_m2,
    "+ Interaction"        = h3_m3,
    "Restricted (conf. 0.85+)" = h3_m4
  ),
  estimate   = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic  = NULL,
  coef_map   = coef_map,
  gof_map    = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars      = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title      = "H3: Foreign expert anti-West framing across topic contexts",
  notes      = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the effect in the domestic/other baseline.",
    "Interaction term shows how this effect differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Column 4 restricted to observations where expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output     = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "           = 1,
      "Main models" = 3,
      "Robustness"  = 1)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "
#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "
#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(5, background = "
#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
all_h3 <- bind_rows(
  lapply(list(h3_m1, h3_m2, h3_m3, h3_m4),
         function(m) tidy(m)) %>%
    bind_rows(.id = "model")
) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, p.value)
print(all_h3, n = 100)

# ── Mention frequency robustness for H3 ───────────────────────────────────

# Reuse expert_article_counts from H1/H2 section
h3_data <- h3_data %>%
  left_join(expert_article_counts, by = "expert_id") %>%
  mutate(inv_freq_weight = 1 / n_articles)

# Rare experts dataset
h3_data_rare <- h3_data %>% filter(n_articles <= 5)

# ── Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Rare experts
h3_m_rare <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                 data   = h3_data_rare,
                 family = binomial)

# Inverse frequency weighted
h3_m_weighted <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                     data    = h3_data,
                     family  = binomial,
                     weights = inv_freq_weight)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "No controls"                = h3_m1,
    "+ Year"                     = h3_m2,
    "+ Interaction"              = h3_m3,
    "Restricted (conf. 0.85+)"   = h3_m4,
    "Rare experts (≤5 articles)" = h3_m_rare,
    "Inverse frequency weighted" = h3_m_weighted
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Mention frequency",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the domestic/other baseline.",
    "Interaction term shows how this differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Rare experts column restricts to experts appearing in 5 or fewer articles.",
    "Weighted column uses inverse article frequency as observation weight.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "           = 1,
      "Main models" = 3,
      "Robustness"  = 3)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(5:7, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
```

### Robustness Ukraine excluded H1 to H3

```{r}
# ── Exclude Ukrainian-attributed experts ───────────────────────────────────

# For H1
h1_data_no_ukr <- h1_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_master != "Ukraine")

h1_m2_no_ukr <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                    data   = h1_data_no_ukr,
                    family = binomial)

# For H2
h2_data_no_ukr <- h2_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_master != "Ukraine")

h2_aw_no_ukr  <- glm(anti_west            ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)
h2_au_no_ukr  <- glm(anti_ukraine         ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)
h2_puk_no_ukr <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)
h2_ar_no_ukr  <- glm(anti_russia          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)
h2_pw_no_ukr  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)
h2_pr_no_ukr  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_ukr, family = binomial)

# For H3
h3_data_no_ukr <- h3_data %>%
  filter(expert_country_master != "Ukraine")

h3_m3_no_ukr <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                    data   = h3_data_no_ukr,
                    family = binomial)

# ── H1 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map_h1 <- c(
  "dominant_topic_cleanInternational geopolitics" = "International geopolitics",
  "dominant_topic_cleanUkraine war"               = "Ukraine war",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMilitary technology"       = "Military technology",
  "dominant_topic_cleanEnergy and sanctions"      = "Energy and sanctions",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMacroeconomy"              = "Macroeconomy",
  "dominant_topic_cleanOpinion and commentary"    = "Opinion and commentary",
  "dominant_topic_cleanInformation services"      = "Information services",
  "dominant_topic_cleanMedia and society"         = "Media and society",
  "dominant_topic_cleanKremlin governance"        = "Kremlin governance",
  "dominant_topic_cleanHousing market"            = "Housing market",
  "dominant_topic_cleanDevelopment and education" = "Development and education",
  "dominant_topic_cleanCOVID-19 and health"       = "COVID-19 and health",
  "dominant_topic_cleanGeneral news"              = "General news",
  "dominant_topic_cleanRegional and local issues" = "Regional and local issues",
  "year"                                          = "Year (linear)"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Year)"        = h1_m2,
    "Excluding Ukrainians" = h1_m2_no_ukr
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h1,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts addresses the systematic",
    "misattribution of Russian domestic experts to Ukraine documented",
    "in the validation analysis (Appendix C).",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",     1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",               15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H2 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

counts_no_ukr <- h2_data_no_ukr %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(anti_west,            na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(anti_ukraine,         na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine, na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(anti_russia,          na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,    na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,  na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_no_ukr_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (excluding Ukrainians): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    counts_no_ukr$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", counts_no_ukr$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  counts_no_ukr$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  counts_no_ukr$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     counts_no_ukr$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   counts_no_ukr$pr,  "."
)

coef_map_h2 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"          = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "year"                    = "Year"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_no_ukr,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_no_ukr,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_no_ukr,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_no_ukr,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_no_ukr,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_no_ukr
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h2,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts addresses the systematic",
    "misattribution of Russian domestic experts to Ukraine documented",
    "in the validation analysis (Appendix C).",
    counts_note,
    counts_no_ukr_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 10
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                            = 1,
      "Main models"                  = 6,
      "Excluding Ukrainians"         = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H3 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map_h3 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                         = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical"                = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Foreign expert x Geopolitical",
  "year"                                   = "Year"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Interaction)"  = h3_m3,
    "Excluding Ukrainians"  = h3_m3_no_ukr
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h3,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the",
    "domestic/other baseline. Interaction term shows how this",
    "differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Excluding Ukrainian-attributed experts addresses the systematic",
    "misattribution of Russian domestic experts to Ukraine documented",
    "in the validation analysis (Appendix C).",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
```

### Robustness academics only H1 to H3

```{r}
# ── Academic experts only robustness ──────────────────────────────────────

# For H1
h1_data_academic <- h1_data %>%
  filter(role_class == "academic_expert")

h1_m2_academic <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                      data   = h1_data_academic,
                      family = binomial)

# For H2
h2_data_academic <- h2_data %>%
  filter(role_class == "academic_expert")

h2_aw_academic  <- glm(anti_west     ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)
h2_au_academic  <- glm(anti_ukraine  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)
h2_puk_academic <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)
h2_ar_academic  <- glm(anti_russia   ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)
h2_pw_academic  <- glm(any_positive_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)
h2_pr_academic  <- glm(any_positive_russia ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                       data = h2_data_academic, family = binomial)

# For H3
h3_data_academic <- h3_data %>%
  filter(role_class == "academic_expert")

h3_m3_academic <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                      data   = h3_data_academic,
                      family = binomial)

# ── H1 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Year)"       = h1_m2,
    "Academic experts only" = h1_m2_academic
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h1,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Academic experts only",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "Academic experts only column restricts to role_class == academic_expert.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",     1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",               15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H2 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

counts_academic <- h2_data_academic %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(anti_west,         na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(anti_ukraine,      na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine,  na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(anti_russia,       na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,     na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,   na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_academic_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (academic experts only): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    counts_academic$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", counts_academic$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  counts_academic$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  counts_academic$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     counts_academic$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   counts_academic$pr,  "."
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_academic,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_academic,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_academic,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_academic,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_academic,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_academic
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h2,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Academic experts only",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Academic experts only columns restrict to role_class == academic_expert.",
    counts_note,
    counts_academic_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 10
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                      = 1,
      "Main models"            = 6,
      "Academic experts only"  = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H3 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Interaction)"  = h3_m3,
    "Academic experts only" = h3_m3_academic
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h3,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Academic experts only",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the",
    "domestic/other baseline. Interaction term shows how this",
    "differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Academic experts only column restricts to role_class == academic_expert.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)


h1_data_rare <- h1_data %>% filter(n_articles <= 5)

h1_m2_rare <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                  data   = h1_data_rare,
                  family = binomial)

h1_m2_rare


```

### Robustness dropped experts treated as Russian H1

```{r}
# ── Prepare dropped experts for H1 ────────────────────────────────────────

# Topic label mapping matching dominant_topic_clean in master_dataset
topic_col_to_label <- c(
  "macro_economy"           = "Macroeconomy",
  "media_society"           = "Media and society",
  "general_news"            = "General news",
  "housing_market"          = "Housing market",
  "opinion_discourse"       = "Opinion and commentary",
  "covid_health"            = "COVID-19 and health",
  "development_education"   = "Development and education",
  "energy_sanctions"        = "Energy and sanctions",
  "military_technology"     = "Military technology",
  "information_services"    = "Information services",
  "kremlin_governance"      = "Kremlin governance",
  "courts_crime"            = "Courts and crime",
  "regions_local"           = "Regional and local issues",
  "ukraine_war"             = "Ukraine war",
  "international_geopolitics" = "International geopolitics"
)

topic_cols <- names(topic_col_to_label)

dropped_h1 <- dropped_topics_k15 %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(format(DateParsed, "%Y")),
    foreign_expert = 0L,
    # Find dominant topic column
    dominant_topic_raw = topic_cols[max.col(across(all_of(topic_cols)))],
    dominant_topic_clean = recode(dominant_topic_raw, !!!topic_col_to_label)
  ) %>%
  select(row_id, year, foreign_expert, dominant_topic_clean)

# ── Combine with h1_data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Keep only the columns needed from h1_data to avoid conflicts
h1_data_slim <- h1_data %>%
  select(row_id, year, foreign_expert, dominant_topic_clean)

h1_data_with_dropped <- bind_rows(
  h1_data_slim,
  dropped_h1
) %>%
  filter(dominant_topic_clean != "No clear topic") %>%
  mutate(
    dominant_topic_clean = relevel(
      factor(dominant_topic_clean),
      ref = "Courts and crime"
    )
  )

# ── Model ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_m2_dropped <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                     data   = h1_data_with_dropped,
                     family = binomial)

# ── Table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Year)"              = h1_m2,
    "Dropped experts as Russian" = h1_m2_dropped
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h1,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Dropped experts treated as Russian",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "Dropped experts column adds approximately 37,000 experts excluded",
    "from the main analysis due to missing country attribution,",
    "treating them as Russian domestic experts (foreign_expert = 0).",
    "This represents the most conservative possible assumption about",
    "the unattributed expert pool.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",     1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",               15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)


```

### Robustness dropped experts treated as Russian H2 and H3

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Load dropped sentiment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

dropped_sentiment <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/olemga/OneDrive - PRIO/Documents/MA Thesis/Methods/Data/lenta_dropped_sentiment_full.csv"
)

library(dplyr)
library(modelsummary)
library(kableExtra)

# ── Topic label mapping ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

topic_col_to_label <- c(
  "macro_economy"             = "Macroeconomy",
  "media_society"             = "Media and society",
  "general_news"              = "General news",
  "housing_market"            = "Housing market",
  "opinion_discourse"         = "Opinion and commentary",
  "covid_health"              = "COVID-19 and health",
  "development_education"     = "Development and education",
  "energy_sanctions"          = "Energy and sanctions",
  "military_technology"       = "Military technology",
  "information_services"      = "Information services",
  "kremlin_governance"        = "Kremlin governance",
  "courts_crime"              = "Courts and crime",
  "regions_local"             = "Regional and local issues",
  "ukraine_war"               = "Ukraine war",
  "international_geopolitics" = "International geopolitics"
)

geopolitical_topics <- c(
  "Ukraine war",
  "International geopolitics",
  "Military technology",
  "Energy and sanctions"
)

topic_cols <- names(topic_col_to_label)

# ── Build topic assignment from dropped_topics_k15 ─────────────────────────

dropped_topics_slim <- dropped_topics_k15 %>%
  mutate(
    row_id               = as.numeric(row_id),
    dominant_topic_raw   = topic_cols[max.col(across(all_of(topic_cols)))],
    dominant_topic_clean = recode(dominant_topic_raw, !!!topic_col_to_label),
    topic_group          = if_else(
      dominant_topic_clean %in% geopolitical_topics,
      "geopolitics",
      "domestic"
    ),
    topic_block = factor(
      if_else(topic_group == "geopolitics", "Geopolitical", "Domestic / other"),
      levels = c("Domestic / other", "Geopolitical")
    )
  ) %>%
  select(row_id, dominant_topic_clean, topic_group, topic_block)

# ── Join topics onto dropped sentiment ────────────────────────────────────

dropped_for_h2 <- dropped_sentiment %>%
  mutate(row_id = as.numeric(row_id)) %>%
  left_join(dropped_topics_slim, by = "row_id") %>%
  mutate(
    foreign_expert = 0L,
    year           = as.integer(format(as.Date(DateParsed), "%Y"))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(year),
    !is.na(topic_block)
  ) %>%
  select(
    row_id, year, foreign_expert, topic_block,
    any_anti_west, any_anti_ukraine, any_positive_ukraine,
    any_anti_russia, any_positive_west, any_positive_russia
  )

# ── Slim h2_data to matching columns ──────────────────────────────────────

h2_data_slim <- h2_data %>%
  rename(
    any_anti_west    = anti_west,
    any_anti_russia  = anti_russia
  ) %>%
  select(
    row_id, year, foreign_expert, topic_block,
    any_anti_west, anti_ukraine, any_positive_ukraine,
    any_anti_russia, any_positive_west, any_positive_russia
  )

# ── Diagnostics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("Row ID overlap check (should be 0):\n")
print(sum(dropped_for_h2$row_id %in% h2_data_slim$row_id))

cat("\ndropped_for_h2 rows:\n")
print(nrow(dropped_for_h2))

cat("\nTopic block distribution in dropped:\n")
print(table(dropped_for_h2$topic_block))

# ── Combine ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h2_data_with_dropped <- bind_rows(h2_data_slim, dropped_for_h2)

cat("\nCombined dataset rows:\n")
print(nrow(h2_data_with_dropped))

cat("\nForeign expert distribution:\n")
print(table(h2_data_with_dropped$foreign_expert))

# ── H2 models ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h2_aw_dropped  <- glm(any_anti_west        ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)
h2_au_dropped  <- glm(anti_ukraine     ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)
h2_puk_dropped <- glm(any_positive_ukraine ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)
h2_ar_dropped  <- glm(any_anti_russia      ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)
h2_pw_dropped  <- glm(any_positive_west    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)
h2_pr_dropped  <- glm(any_positive_russia  ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data_with_dropped, family = binomial)

# ── H3 model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h3_m3_dropped <- glm(any_anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                     data   = h2_data_with_dropped,
                     family = binomial)

# ── Outcome counts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

counts_dropped_h2 <- h2_data_with_dropped %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(any_anti_west,          na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(any_anti_ukraine,       na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine,   na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(any_anti_russia,        na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,      na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,    na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_dropped_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (with dropped experts as Russian): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    counts_dropped_h2$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", counts_dropped_h2$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  counts_dropped_h2$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  counts_dropped_h2$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     counts_dropped_h2$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   counts_dropped_h2$pr,  "."
)

# ── Coefficient maps ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

coef_map_h2 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"          = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "year"                    = "Year"
)

coef_map_h3 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                         = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical"                = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "foreign_expert:topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Foreign expert x Geopolitical",
  "year"                                   = "Year"
)

# ── H2 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_dropped,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_dropped,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_dropped,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_dropped,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_dropped,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_dropped
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h2,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Dropped experts treated as Russian",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Dropped experts column adds experts excluded from the main",
    "analysis due to missing country attribution, treating them",
    "as Russian domestic experts. This is the most conservative",
    "possible assumption about the unattributed expert pool.",
    counts_note,
    counts_dropped_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 10
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                          = 1,
      "Main models"                = 6,
      "Dropped experts as Russian" = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H3 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Interaction)"       = h3_m3,
    "Dropped experts as Russian" = h3_m3_dropped
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h3,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Dropped experts treated as Russian",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the",
    "domestic/other baseline. Interaction term shows how this",
    "differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Dropped experts treated as Russian domestic experts.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
```

### Robustness: alternative explanations H1 H3

```{r}
# ── H1: Alternative specifications ────────────────────────────────────────

# Probit
h1_probit <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                 data   = h1_data,
                 family = binomial(link = "probit"))

# Linear probability model
h1_lpm <- lm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
             data = h1_data)

# ── H1 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Logit (main)"  = h1_m2,
    "Probit"        = h1_probit,
    "LPM"           = h1_lpm
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h1,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik", "r.squared"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Alternative model specifications",
  notes     = c(
    "Logit and probit coefficients are log-odds and probit coefficients",
    "respectively. LPM coefficients are percentage point differences.",
    "Standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",     1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",               15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                             = 1,
      "Main"                          = 1,
      "Alternative specifications"    = 2)
  ) %>%
  column_spec(3:4, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H2: Alternative specifications ────────────────────────────────────────

# Probit models
h2_aw_probit  <- glm(anti_west          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))
h2_au_probit  <- glm(anti_ukraine       ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
```
