```{r}
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))
h2_puk_probit <- glm(any_positive_ukraine   ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))
h2_ar_probit  <- glm(anti_russia        ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))
h2_pw_probit  <- glm(any_positive_west      ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))
h2_pr_probit  <- glm(any_positive_russia    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data, family = binomial(link = "probit"))

# LPM models
h2_aw_lpm  <- lm(anti_west          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)
h2_au_lpm  <- lm(anti_ukraine       ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)
h2_puk_lpm <- lm(any_positive_ukraine   ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)
h2_ar_lpm  <- lm(anti_russia        ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)
h2_pw_lpm  <- lm(any_positive_west      ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)
h2_pr_lpm  <- lm(any_positive_russia    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                 data = h2_data)

# ── H2 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_probit,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_probit,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_probit,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_probit,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_probit,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_probit,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_lpm,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_lpm,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_lpm,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_lpm,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_lpm,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_lpm
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h2,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Alternative model specifications",
  notes     = c(
    "Logit and probit report log-odds and probit coefficients respectively.",
    "LPM reports percentage point differences.",
    "Standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    counts_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 9
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                          = 1,
      "Logit (main)"               = 6,
      "Probit"                     = 6,
      "LPM"                        = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13,  background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(14:19, background = "#f0f0f0", italic = TRUE)

# ── H3: Alternative specifications ────────────────────────────────────────

# Probit
h3_probit <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                 data   = h3_data,
                 family = binomial(link = "probit"))

# LPM
h3_lpm <- lm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
             data = h3_data)

# ── H3 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Logit (main)" = h3_m3,
    "Probit"       = h3_probit,
    "LPM"          = h3_lpm
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h3,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik", "r.squared"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Alternative model specifications",
  notes     = c(
    "Logit and probit report log-odds and probit coefficients respectively.",
    "LPM reports percentage point differences.",
    "Standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the",
    "domestic/other baseline. Interaction term shows how this",
    "differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                          = 1,
      "Main"                       = 1,
      "Alternative specifications" = 2)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(3:4, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
```

### Robustness: exclude government official h1 h3

```{r}
# ── Exclude government officials ───────────────────────────────────────────

h1_data_no_gov <- h1_data %>%
  filter(role_class != "government_official")

h2_data_no_gov <- h2_data %>%
  filter(role_class != "government_official")

h3_data_no_gov <- h3_data %>%
  filter(role_class != "government_official")

# ── H1 model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h1_m2_no_gov <- glm(foreign_expert ~ dominant_topic_clean + year,
                    data   = h1_data_no_gov,
                    family = binomial)

# ── H2 models ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h2_aw_no_gov  <- glm(anti_west          ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)
h2_au_no_gov  <- glm(anti_ukraine       ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)
h2_puk_no_gov <- glm(any_positive_ukraine   ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)
h2_ar_no_gov  <- glm(anti_russia        ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)
h2_pw_no_gov  <- glm(any_positive_west      ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)
h2_pr_no_gov  <- glm(any_positive_russia    ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                     data = h2_data_no_gov, family = binomial)

# ── H3 model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

h3_m3_no_gov <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                    data   = h3_data_no_gov,
                    family = binomial)

# ── Sample size check ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("H1 sample sizes:\n")
cat("Main:", nrow(h1_data), "\n")
cat("No government officials:", nrow(h1_data_no_gov), "\n")
cat("Removed:", nrow(h1_data) - nrow(h1_data_no_gov), "\n")

cat("\nH2 sample sizes:\n")
cat("Main:", nrow(h2_data), "\n")
cat("No government officials:", nrow(h2_data_no_gov), "\n")

# ── Outcome counts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

counts_no_gov <- h2_data_no_gov %>%
  summarise(
    aw  = sum(anti_west,          na.rm = TRUE),
    au  = sum(anti_ukraine,       na.rm = TRUE),
    puk = sum(any_positive_ukraine,   na.rm = TRUE),
    ar  = sum(anti_russia,        na.rm = TRUE),
    pw  = sum(any_positive_west,      na.rm = TRUE),
    pr  = sum(any_positive_russia,    na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_no_gov_note <- paste0(
  "Outcome event counts (excluding government officials): ",
  "Anti-West = ",    counts_no_gov$aw,  ", ",
  "Anti-Ukraine = ", counts_no_gov$au,  ", ",
  "Pro-Ukraine = ",  counts_no_gov$puk, ", ",
  "Anti-Russia = ",  counts_no_gov$ar,  ", ",
  "Pro-West = ",     counts_no_gov$pw,  ", ",
  "Pro-Russia = ",   counts_no_gov$pr,  "."
)

# ── H1 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Year)"                  = h1_m2,
    "Excluding government officials" = h1_m2_no_gov
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h1,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H1 Robustness: Excluding government officials",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: Courts and crime topic.",
    "Excluding government officials removes role_class == government_official.",
    "Government officials are not expert voices in the journalistic sense",
    "and their inclusion may conflate editorial selection of expert credibility",
    "with routine citation of official sources.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  pack_rows("Geopolitical topics",     1,  4, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  pack_rows("Domestic / other topics", 5, 14, bold = TRUE, background = "#f7f0e8") %>%
  pack_rows("Controls",               15, 15, bold = FALSE, background = "#f5f5f5") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H2 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr,
    "Anti-West"    = h2_aw_no_gov,
    "Anti-Ukraine" = h2_au_no_gov,
    "Pro-Ukraine"  = h2_puk_no_gov,
    "Anti-Russia"  = h2_ar_no_gov,
    "Pro-West"     = h2_pw_no_gov,
    "Pro-Russia"   = h2_pr_no_gov
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h2,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H2 Robustness: Excluding government officials",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Excluding government officials removes role_class == government_official.",
    counts_note,
    counts_no_gov_note,
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 10
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                              = 1,
      "Main models"                    = 6,
      "Excluding government officials" = 6)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(8:13, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)

# ── H3 table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (+ Interaction)"           = h3_m3,
    "Excluding government officials" = h3_m3_no_gov
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h3,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 Robustness: Excluding government officials",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, domestic/other topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the association in the",
    "domestic/other baseline. Interaction term shows how this",
    "differs in geopolitical topics.",
    "Excluding government officials removes role_class == government_official.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(3, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE)
```

### Robustness h4

```{r}

# Restrict to post-2014 for main H4 models
h4_data <- h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2014)

# Alternative cutoff: 2013
h4_data_2013 <- h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2013)

# Alternative cutoff: 2015  
h4_data_2015 <- h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2015)

# Exclude post-2022
h4_data_no2022 <- h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2014 & year < 2022)

# Pre-2014 placebo
h4_data_pre2014 <- h2_data %>% filter(year < 2014)

# Models: anti-West outcome only since that is the H4 focus
h4_main    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data = h4_data, family = binomial)
h4_2013    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data = h4_data_2013, family = binomial)
h4_2015    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data = h4_data_2015, family = binomial)
h4_no2022  <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data = h4_data_no2022, family = binomial)
h4_placebo <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data = h4_data_pre2014, family = binomial)


summary(h4_main)
summary(h4_2013)
summary(h4_2015)
summary(h4_no2022)
summary(h4_placebo)

# Check variable name consistency
h4_data_pre2014 <- h2_data %>% filter(year < 2014)

# Interaction model on full dataset
h4_interaction <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * post_2014 + 
                        topic_block + year,
                      data = h2_data %>% 
                        mutate(post_2014 = as.integer(year >= 2014)),
                      family = binomial)

summary(h4_interaction)

# ── Split by topic block and period ───────────────────────────────────────

# Geopolitical topics only
h4_geo_pre2014  <- h2_data %>% 
  filter(year < 2014, topic_block == "Geopolitical")

h4_geo_post2014 <- h2_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014, topic_block == "Geopolitical")

# Domestic topics only
h4_dom_pre2014  <- h2_data %>% 
  filter(year < 2014, topic_block == "Domestic / other")

h4_dom_post2014 <- h2_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014, topic_block == "Domestic / other")

# Models
h4_geo_pre  <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
                   data = h4_geo_pre2014,  family = binomial)
h4_geo_post <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
                   data = h4_geo_post2014, family = binomial)
h4_dom_pre  <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
                   data = h4_dom_pre2014,  family = binomial)
h4_dom_post <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year,
                   data = h4_dom_post2014, family = binomial)

summary(h4_geo_pre)
summary(h4_geo_post)
summary(h4_dom_pre)
summary(h4_dom_post)

coef_map_h4 <- c(
  "foreign_expert"          = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockGeopolitical" = "Topic: Geopolitical",
  "year"                    = "Year"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Geopolitical pre-2014"  = h4_geo_pre,
    "Geopolitical post-2014" = h4_geo_post,
    "Domestic pre-2014"      = h4_dom_pre,
    "Domestic post-2014"     = h4_dom_post
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h4,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H4 Robustness: Foreign expert anti-West coefficient by topic block and period",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Outcome: anti-West sentiment. Baseline: domestic expert.",
    "Topic block fixed within each model — geopolitical models",
    "include only geopolitical articles, domestic models include",
    "only domestic and other articles.",
    "Pre-2014 period uses editorially independent Lenta.ru as baseline.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "              = 1,
      "Geopolitical"   = 2,
      "Domestic"       = 2)
  ) %>%
  column_spec(2:3, background = "#e8f0f7") %>%
  column_spec(4:5, background = "#f7f0e8")

# ── Periodization checks on full dataset ──────────────────────────────────

h4_main    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data   = h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2014),
                  family = binomial)

h4_2013    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data   = h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2013),
                  family = binomial)

h4_2015    <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data   = h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2015),
                  family = binomial)

h4_no2022  <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data   = h2_data %>% filter(year >= 2014 & year < 2022),
                  family = binomial)

h4_placebo <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + topic_block + year,
                  data   = h2_data %>% filter(year < 2014),
                  family = binomial)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "Main (2014--2024)"  = h4_main,
    "Alt. cutoff 2013"   = h4_2013,
    "Alt. cutoff 2015"   = h4_2015,
    "Excl. 2022--2024"   = h4_no2022,
    "Pre-2014 placebo"   = h4_placebo
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_h4,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H4 Robustness: Periodization checks",
  notes     = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Outcome: anti-West sentiment. Baseline: domestic expert,",
    "domestic/other topic.",
    "Main model restricted to 2014 onward. Alternative cutoff",
    "models use 2013 and 2015 as alternative start dates.",
    "Excl. 2022-2024 removes the acute war period.",
    "Pre-2014 placebo uses the editorially independent period.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output    = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width        = FALSE,
    font_size         = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" "                    = 1,
      "Periodization checks" = 3,
      "Exclusion"            = 1,
      "Placebo"              = 1)
  ) %>%
  column_spec(5, background = "#fafafa", italic = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(6, background = "#f0f0f0", italic = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# ── Step 1: Total articles per month from full corpus ──────────────────────

articles_per_month <- lenta_news_1999_2024 %>%
  mutate(
    date  = as.Date(date),
    month = floor_date(date, "month")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(month)) %>%
  count(month, name = "n_articles_total")

# ── Step 2: Articles with foreign and domestic experts per month ──────────
# Count distinct articles (row_id) per month for each expert type

articles_with_experts <- master_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    date  = as.Date(date),
    month = floor_date(date, "month")
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(month)) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    n_articles_foreign  = n_distinct(row_id[foreign_expert == 1]),
    n_articles_domestic = n_distinct(row_id[foreign_expert == 0]),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ── Step 3: Combine and compute shares ────────────────────────────────────

monthly_shares <- articles_per_month %>%
  left_join(articles_with_experts, by = "month") %>%
  mutate(
    n_articles_foreign  = ifelse(is.na(n_articles_foreign),  0, n_articles_foreign),
    n_articles_domestic = ifelse(is.na(n_articles_domestic), 0, n_articles_domestic),
    share_foreign       = n_articles_foreign  / n_articles_total,
    share_domestic      = n_articles_domestic / n_articles_total
  ) %>%
  filter(month >= as.Date("1999-09-01") & month <= as.Date("2024-06-30"))

# ── Step 4: Pivot for plotting ────────────────────────────────────────────

plot_data <- monthly_shares %>%
  select(month, share_foreign, share_domestic) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(share_foreign, share_domestic),
    names_to  = "expert_type",
    values_to = "share"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Expert = factor(expert_type,
                    levels = c("share_domestic", "share_foreign"),
                    labels = c("Domestic", "Foreign"))
  )

# ── Step 5: Plot in Figure 6.4 style ──────────────────────────────────────

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = month, y = share, color = Expert)) +
  
  # Raw monthly line (light)
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
  
  # Smoothed trend
  geom_smooth(
    method    = "loess",
    span      = 0.15,
    se        = FALSE,
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  # 2014 editorial change
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2014-03-01"),
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = as.Date("2014-03-01"),
    y        = Inf,
    label    = "Editorial change",
    angle    = 90,
    vjust    = -0.4,
    hjust    = 1.1,
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey40",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  
  # 2020 COVID-19
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2020-03-01"),
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = as.Date("2020-03-01"),
    y        = Inf,
    label    = "COVID-19",
    angle    = 90,
    vjust    = -0.4,
    hjust    = 1.1,
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey40",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  
  # 2022 invasion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "grey50",
    linewidth  = 0.5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x        = as.Date("2022-02-24"),
    y        = Inf,
    label    = "Ukraine invasion",
    angle    = 90,
    vjust    = -0.4,
    hjust    = 1.1,
    size     = 3,
    color    = "grey40",
    fontface = "italic"
  ) +
  
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Domestic" = "#b04a4a", "Foreign" = "#2b6cb0"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "3 years",
    date_labels = "%Y"
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  
  labs(
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Share of articles with expert mention",
    title    = "Expert use as share of total articles published",
    subtitle = "Monthly shares with smoothed trends. Dashed lines mark key editorial and geopolitical events."
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

### new H3

```{r}
library(broom)  # for tidy() in the diagnostic dump at the end

# ── Additional data prep ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Stratified subsets
h3_domestic              <- h3_data            %>% filter(topic_block == "Domestic / other")
h3_geopolitical          <- h3_data            %>% filter(topic_block == "Geopolitical")
h3_domestic_restricted   <- h3_data_restricted %>% filter(topic_block == "Domestic / other")
h3_geopolitical_restricted <- h3_data_restricted %>% filter(topic_block == "Geopolitical")

# Reparameterized data: geopolitical as reference category
h3_data_georef            <- h3_data %>%
  mutate(topic_block = factor(topic_block,
                              levels = c("Geopolitical", "Domestic / other")))
h3_data_georef_restricted <- h3_data_restricted %>%
  mutate(topic_block = factor(topic_block,
                              levels = c("Geopolitical", "Domestic / other")))


# ── Stratified models (headline H3 table) ─────────────────────────────────

h3_dom_m1 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert,        data = h3_domestic,            family = binomial)
h3_dom_m2 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year, data = h3_domestic,            family = binomial)
h3_dom_m3 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year, data = h3_domestic_restricted, family = binomial)

h3_geo_m1 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert,        data = h3_geopolitical,            family = binomial)
h3_geo_m2 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year, data = h3_geopolitical,            family = binomial)
h3_geo_m3 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert + year, data = h3_geopolitical_restricted, family = binomial)

coef_map_strat <- c(
  "foreign_expert" = "Foreign expert",
  "year"           = "Year"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "(1)" = h3_dom_m1, "(2)" = h3_dom_m2, "(3)" = h3_dom_m3,
    "(4)" = h3_geo_m1, "(5)" = h3_geo_m2, "(6)" = h3_geo_m3
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_strat,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3: Foreign expert anti-West framing, stratified by topic block",
  notes = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Each column is a separate logistic regression on the indicated subset.",
    "Columns (1)-(3): articles in domestic / other topics (economic, health, legal, regional, etc.).",
    "Columns (4)-(6): articles in geopolitical topics (international geopolitics, Ukraine war, security).",
    "Columns (3) and (6) restricted to expert_country_maxconf >= 0.85.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 1, "Domestic / other topics" = 3, "Geopolitical topics" = 3)
  ) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7")  # foreign_expert row


# ── Reparameterized interaction model (geopolitical as baseline) ──────────

h3_re_m3 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                data = h3_data_georef,            family = binomial)
h3_re_m4 <- glm(anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
                data = h3_data_georef_restricted, family = binomial)

coef_map_re <- c(
  "foreign_expert"                              = "Foreign expert",
  "topic_blockDomestic / other"                 = "Topic: Domestic / other",
  "foreign_expert:topic_blockDomestic / other"  = "Foreign expert x Domestic",
  "year"                                        = "Year"
)

modelsummary(
  list(
    "+ Interaction"           = h3_re_m3,
    "Restricted (conf >=0.85)" = h3_re_m4
  ),
  estimate  = "{estimate} ({std.error}){stars}",
  statistic = NULL,
  coef_map  = coef_map_re,
  gof_map   = c("nobs", "logLik"),
  stars     = c("*" = 0.05, "**" = 0.01, "***" = 0.001),
  title     = "H3 (reparameterized): geopolitical topic as reference category",
  notes = c(
    "Log-odds coefficients with standard errors in parentheses.",
    "Baseline: domestic expert, geopolitical topic.",
    "Foreign expert coefficient captures the foreign-expert effect in geopolitical topics.",
    "Interaction term shows the additional foreign-expert premium when moving into domestic / other topics.",
    "* p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001"
  ),
  output = "kableExtra"
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 12
  ) %>%
  row_spec(3, bold = TRUE, background = "#e8f0f7")  # interaction row


# ── Diagnostic dump ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

all_h3_strat <- bind_rows(
  lapply(
    list(
      h3_dom_m1 = h3_dom_m1, h3_dom_m2 = h3_dom_m2, h3_dom_m3 = h3_dom_m3,
      h3_geo_m1 = h3_geo_m1, h3_geo_m2 = h3_geo_m2, h3_geo_m3 = h3_geo_m3,
      h3_re_m3  = h3_re_m3,  h3_re_m4  = h3_re_m4
    ),
    tidy
  ),
  .id = "model"
) %>%
  select(model, term, estimate, std.error, p.value)

print(all_h3_strat, n = 100)
```

```{r}
library(marginaleffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# ── Model used for predictions ────────────────────────────────────────────
# Original parameterization (domestic-block as baseline) — matches the
# interaction model in the thesis. Predictions are identical regardless of
# which factor level is reference; using this version for continuity.

h3_pred_model <- glm(
  anti_west ~ foreign_expert * topic_block + year,
  data = h3_data,
  family = binomial
)


# ── Predicted probabilities, averaged across observed year distribution ──

preds <- avg_predictions(
  h3_pred_model,
  by = c("foreign_expert", "topic_block")
) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    expert_label = factor(
      if_else(foreign_expert == 1, "Foreign expert", "Domestic expert"),
      levels = c("Domestic expert", "Foreign expert")
    ),
    topic_block = factor(
      topic_block,
      levels = c("Domestic / other", "Geopolitical")
    )
  )

print(preds)


# ── Within-topic contrasts (foreign minus domestic, in pp) ───────────────

contrasts_h3 <- avg_comparisons(
  h3_pred_model,
  variables = "foreign_expert",
  by = "topic_block"
) %>%
  as_tibble()

print(contrasts_h3)


# ── Figure ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

p_h3 <- ggplot(
  preds,
  aes(x = topic_block, y = estimate,
      fill = expert_label, group = expert_label)
) +
  geom_col(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    colour = "grey25"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", estimate * 100)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    vjust = -1.6,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "grey20"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Domestic expert" = "#8896a3",
               "Foreign expert"  = "#b04a3f"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.20))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Predicted probability of anti-West sentiment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "top",
    legend.key.size    = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.margin        = margin(10, 14, 10, 10)
  )

print(p_h3)


# ── Save (adjust path / dimensions to match your other figures) ──────────

ggsave("fig_h3_predicted_probabilities.png",
      p_h3, width = 5.5, height = 3.6, device = cairo_pdf)
```
